title: Quintinia neoebudica
taxonomy: Quintinia neoebudica is classified within the family Cephalotaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the snake bark trees. The genus Quintinia is part of the Cephalotaceae family and is native to the Pacific Islands. Quintinia neoebudica is a specific species within this genus. The classification of Quintinia neoebudica is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphological features.

intro: Quintinia neoebudica, also known as Ebullian, is a plant species native to Vanuatu, a Pacific island nation. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and infections, due to its reported health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The bioactive constituents of Quintinia neoebudica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Quintinia neoebudica is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms associated with fever and respiratory infections.
